rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks in valid component or implementation scopes
Place component type annexes in component type scopes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity by respecting AADL scope rules for annex insertion
Ensure generated AGREE contracts are valid in their target scope
Preserve existing AADL structure when inserting AGREE annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees referring to component ports in implementation scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not insert AGREE annex blocks in scopes where they would violate AADL architectural rules
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
